The College of Management - Shaping the Future of Higher Education in Israel

For over 45 years, the College of Management has been at the forefront of higher education in Israel. As the first private academic institution recognized by the Council for Higher Education, we continue to lead in innovation and adaptation to the evolving needs of the job market.

9,000   students | 85,000 alumni | Diverse Bachelor's and Master's programs

Connected to industry: Curricula tailored to current market demands

Advanced campus in Rishon LeZion, central Israel

At the College of Management, we are preparing Israel's future leaders. Join us and be at the forefront of academic innovation and excellence!

resumePosition.details:
• Led a team of 3 engineers with diverse backgrounds, fostering collaboration across diverse skill levels.
• Led the development of an innovative, first-of-its-kind modular 33,000V cell for the company, designed for a 5MW outdoor utility mass production inverter (10K annually), for an automated assembly line.
• The inverter features an IP65/PD1 rating, disperses 80kW of heat, and handles 5,000A.
• Designed and led the entire project lifecycle, from architectural design to prototype testing of 1MW racks in the lab.
• Managed on-premise problem-solving to ensure the design met functional and performance requirements.
• Designed the transformer using unique composite materials like GPO3, insulating the secondary windings with Epoxy resin, and ensuring thermal air cooling while maintaining the required creepage-clearance distances for 33kV.
• Developed an internal automated tool to streamline BOM tracking, part order monitoring, and Gantt chart creation, improving managerial efficiency.
• Designed the company’s focal point project, AC EV Charger, IP65 and Nema 3R ratings, involving design for die-cast enclosure, injection molded plastic (Polycarbonate) parts and a transparent plastic light-guide, optimized with a light rendering software to create the industrial-design’s required effect and snap calculations for easy assembly, allowing installment on the wall using sheet metal manufactured bracket, keeping tight tolerance stacks in order to support mass production volumes of 100K annually.
• Designed the mass production commercial inverter’s main, digital and power PCBs collaborating closely with layout engineers to ensure mechanical compatibility, as well as 3 heatsink extrusions to support a range of different kW outputs for the residential inverter.
• Led mechanical design support for residential inverter and storage system, involving die-cast parts and 3D-sealed IP67 enclosures, adapting to evolving designs and delivering solutions quickly, as well as incorporating ultrasonic welding for sealed assembly, with iterative testing using prototyping technologies, from design to DFM and mold manufacturing.
• Designed communication PCBs implemented across the company's entire product line.
• Managed the full part lifecycle process: Design, mechanical drawings, ECO, revision management, first article inspections, mold approvals, and more.
• Managed the design lifecycle: PDR, DR, and CDR stages for product assemblies, ensuring alignment with New Product Introduction teams.
• Created an intra-organizational mechanical items cost prediction tool.
• Engineered a semi-automated jig for Automated Test Equipment department’s requirements, integrating pogo pins and linear rails for accurate testing.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Afeka Tel Aviv Academic College of Engineering
Welcome to the Afeka Tel Aviv Academic College of Engineering!

Located at the heart of the Israeli high-tech industry in Tel Aviv, the Afeka Academic College of Engineering has positioned itself as one of the leading institutions for engineering studies in Israel. Afeka offers a strong and cutting-edge academic program that produces highly skilled and professional engineers. The exceptional level of training, coupled with Afeka's strong ties to the industry, paves the way for Afeka graduates to position themselves at the forefront of the Israeli and International engineering community.

The Afeka Academic College of Engineering is accredited by the Israel Council for Higher Education and offers B.Sc. degrees in the following fields of engineering:

Medical Engineering
Electrical Engineering
Mechanical Engineering
Industrial Engineering and Management
Software Engineering
Computer Science
Data Science


M.Sc. Program in:
Medical Engineering
Intelligent Systems
Systems Engineering
Energy and Power Systems Engineering
Industrial engineering and management

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
ORT colleges are part of ORT Israel, which was established in 1949 and now includes 150 educational institutions. The network trains students to cope with the accelerated rate of development in the economy and in industry.

ORT colleges devise study curricula adapted to the needs of the country and to future needs, in collaboration with other bodies, such as: the Ministry of Science and Technology, the Ministry of Industry, Trade & Employment, the IDF, high-tech industries and other leading industries in the national economy, both in the private and public sectors.

Our graduates are integrated in the employment market in a wide range of key positions, in high-tech industries, other leading industries, government ministries, in the private market and in a large number of places of employment that require high quality professionals in the technological fields, at all levels.

ORT colleges operate as interdisciplinary education centers, ranging from science and technology studies to the arts, design and communications. The colleges aim to provide a solution for every student, enabling them to successfully meet local and global technological requirements.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: ORT Colleges

companyResolutionResult.description: Electreon is the leading provider of wireless charging solutions for electric vehicles (EVs), providing end-to-end charging infrastructure and services to meet the needs and efficiency demands of shared, public and commercial fleet operators and consumers. The company’s proprietary inductive technology dynamically (while in motion) and statically (while stopped) charges EVs quickly and safely, eliminating range anxiety, lowering total costs of EV ownership, and reducing battery capacity needs—making it one of the most environmentally sustainable, scalable, and compelling charging solutions available today. Electreon works with cities and fleet operators on a charging as a service (CaaS) platform that enables cost-effective electrification of public, commercial, and autonomous fleets for smooth and continuous operation. For more information, visit Electreon.com.
companyResolutionResult.name: Electreon

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Welcome to the Jerusalem College of Technology (JCT), a leading institution where technological innovation meets traditional Judaic studies in the heart of Jerusalem. At JCT, we provide world-class education in technology, health sciences and business, coupled with a rich Judaic studies program that prepares students for leadership in a globalized world.

About Us:

At JCT, we are committed to excellence in education. Our programs in engineering, computer science, health sciences, business, and more are designed to equip students with cutting-edge technical skills and a strong ethical foundation. Our unique blend of modern technology and Jewish values offers a distinctive educational experience.

Why choose JCT?

Top-Tier Education: Our esteemed faculty deliver high-quality instruction in various fields, preparing our students for their future careers.

Comprehensive Judaic Studies: Students don’t have to choose between academic excellence and spiritual growth. Our Judaic studies program is designed to enhance both. Our top-level shiurim and rich religious environment allow students to deepen their spiritual and ethical development while pursuing their academic and professional goals.

Strategic Location: Situated in the historic and vibrant city of Jerusalem, JCT provides a stimulating academic environment enriched by cultural, spiritual and intellectual opportunities.

Supportive Community: We offer a collaborative community where students, faculty, and alumni support each other. Our focus on mentorship and career development ensures that students have the resources they need to excel.

Connect with us to learn more about our programs, events, and opportunities.

Stay Connected: Follow our LinkedIn page for updates, news, and insights. Visit our website or contact us for more information.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In the academic environment in Israel, the Open University of Israel is similar to other universities in its pursuit of excellence and its commitment to superior scientific and scholastic standards. However, it differs in its organizational structure, its curriculum, and its admission requirements. 

The Open University is open to all those who wish to study a single course or a battery of courses, or to pursue a full program of study toward a Bachelor's degree. Enrollment does not require matriculation or any other certificate from another educational institution. Though applicants are not required to provide proof of prior scholastic achievements, their academic achievements are the key to their success at the Open University. Along with its open admission policy, the Open University demands of its students personal qualities without which academic achievements cannot be attained: ambition, motivation, perseverance, responsibility and self-discipline. Students are required to study on their own, practice at home, actively participate in group learning activities, and meet course deadlines and requirements. 

Since 1996, the Open University has offered programs of study leading to a Master's degree. In contrast to the open admission policy for undergraduate studies, admission to graduate studies is contingent on fulfillment of certain requirements. 
The Open University is flexible in structure and organization. Applicants joining the Open University student body are not required to enroll in a faculty or department, but rather in specific courses. An individual enrolled in a course becomes a student of the Open University. 

The structure of studies at the Open University also differs from the structure customary at other universities, as it is not defined by years. Open University students are not first, second or third year students. Student are entitled to determine their own rate of progress and the degree of progress in not measured by units of time ...
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Open University of Israel

The Directorate's main objectives are to initiate, develop, and strengthen the IDF's technological systems. With growing technology, the Directorate has become a center and leader in combating cyber threats towards Israel.
companyResolutionResult.name: J6 & Cyber Defense Directorate, IDF

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Academic excellence and contribution to the local community have been at the heart of the College's vision since its establishment. The Academic College of Tel Aviv-Yafo was founded in 1994 as a non-profit public institution, and in under three decades has made substantial progress in realizing its vision.
 
The College's six schools offer nine Bachelors programs and ten Masters programs. 4,780 students are currently enrolled in the College, which already boasts nearly 19,000 graduates. Our alumni have amassed significant achievements in academia and business, as well as civic activities and public initiatives. 
 
The schools of Management & Economics, Computer Sciences, Behavioral Sciences, Nursing Sciences, Government & Society, and Information Systems offer students a range of study programs that encourage inspiring and collaborative dialog among various disciplines.
 
The College’s curricula are rigorous and challenging, and the faculty is committed to high quality teaching and active, ongoing research. The College's "open door" policy helps students cope with both the demanding study requirements as well as with personal problems.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The "IDF School of Computer Science," also known as "Basmach," is a program within the Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) that provides advanced training and education in computer science and related fields to soldiers who have been identified as having exceptional talent in these areas. The program was established in the 1980s as a response to the growing importance of computing and technology in the military.
Soldiers who are accepted into the Basmach program receive intensive training in computer science, software engineering, cybersecurity, and other related fields, as well as practical experience working on real-world military technology projects. The program is highly selective and competitive, and graduates are highly sought after by leading technology companies and startups in Israel and around the world.
The Basmach program is part of a larger trend in the Israeli military of investing in high-tech capabilities and developing the next generation of military technology leaders. Through programs like Basmach, the IDF aims to stay at the forefront of technological innovation and maintain its strategic military advantage in a rapidly changing global security environment.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Basmach - IDF School of Computer Science

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ben-Gurion University of the Negev is one of Israel’s leading research universities and among the world leaders in many fields. 

It has around 20,000 students and 4,000 faculty members in the Faculties of Engineering Sciences; Health Sciences; Natural Sciences; the Pinchas Sapir Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences; the Guilford Glazer Faculty of Business and Management; the Joyce and Irving Goldman School of Medicine; the Kreitman School of Advanced Graduate Studies; and the Albert Katz International School for Desert Studies. More than 100,000 alumni play important roles in all areas of research and development, industry, health care, the economy, society, culture and education in Israel.

The University has three main campuses: The Marcus Family Campus in Beer-Sheva; the research campus at Sede Boqer and the Eilat Campus, and is home to national and multi-disciplinary research institutes: the National Institute for Biotechnology in the Negev; the National Institute of Solar Energy; the Ilse Katz Institute for Nanoscale Science and Technology; the Jacob Blaustein Institutes for Desert Research; the Ben-Gurion Research Institute for the Study of Israel & Zionism, and Heksherim - The Research Institute for Jewish and Israeli Literature and Culture.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Ben-Gurion University of the Negev

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Approximately 36 years before Israel declared independence, a ceremony took place on the barren slopes of Mount Carmel near the Port of Haifa, which was then occupied by the Ottoman Empire. Unknown to the Haifa community witnessing the event, this would be a milestone in history. This first cornerstone embodied an implausible vision of creating a world-class institute of scientific and technological education in the Holy Land.
The story of the “Technikum” – the original German name of the Technion – is a tale of the century. The second industrial revolution created the printing presses and communications infrastructure allowing Jews scattered around the globe to organize in face of rising anti-Semitism. As Jews were often barred from technical education, the establishment of a technical school was a first priority to rebuilding a Jewish homeland. The Technion was to become a unique worldwide university that would precede, create, shape, and protect a modern state.
The cornerstone laid on April 11, 1912, set in motion a century of progress responding to national and global needs. Technion – Israel Institute of Technology would grow rapidly, becoming a global pioneer in fields such as biotechnology, stem cell research, space, computer science, nanotechnology, and energy. 
Four Technion professors have won Nobel Prizes. As it approaches its centennial in 2025 – 100 years after the first class took place – Technion City is a thriving world center of trailblazing excellence in research and teaching for the benefit of humanity, with 18 faculties, more than 14,000 students, 135 academic programs, and 60 research institutes. Since the Technion opened its doors, more than 90,000 Technion alumni have played a huge part in creating and leading the ‘Startup Nation,’ impacting the lives of millions of people worldwide.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Technion - Israel Institute of Technology

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
In the academic environment in Israel, the Open University of Israel is similar to other universities in its pursuit of excellence and its commitment to superior scientific and scholastic standards. However, it differs in its organizational structure, its curriculum, and its admission requirements. 

The Open University is open to all those who wish to study a single course or a battery of courses, or to pursue a full program of study toward a Bachelor's degree. Enrollment does not require matriculation or any other certificate from another educational institution. Though applicants are not required to provide proof of prior scholastic achievements, their academic achievements are the key to their success at the Open University. Along with its open admission policy, the Open University demands of its students personal qualities without which academic achievements cannot be attained: ambition, motivation, perseverance, responsibility and self-discipline. Students are required to study on their own, practice at home, actively participate in group learning activities, and meet course deadlines and requirements. 

Since 1996, the Open University has offered programs of study leading to a Master's degree. In contrast to the open admission policy for undergraduate studies, admission to graduate studies is contingent on fulfillment of certain requirements. 
The Open University is flexible in structure and organization. Applicants joining the Open University student body are not required to enroll in a faculty or department, but rather in specific courses. An individual enrolled in a course becomes a student of the Open University. 

The structure of studies at the Open University also differs from the structure customary at other universities, as it is not defined by years. Open University students are not first, second or third year students. Student are entitled to determine their own rate of progress and the degree of progress in not measured by units of time ...
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Open University of Israel

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
ORT colleges are part of ORT Israel, which was established in 1949 and now includes 150 educational institutions. The network trains students to cope with the accelerated rate of development in the economy and in industry.

ORT colleges devise study curricula adapted to the needs of the country and to future needs, in collaboration with other bodies, such as: the Ministry of Science and Technology, the Ministry of Industry, Trade & Employment, the IDF, high-tech industries and other leading industries in the national economy, both in the private and public sectors.

Our graduates are integrated in the employment market in a wide range of key positions, in high-tech industries, other leading industries, government ministries, in the private market and in a large number of places of employment that require high quality professionals in the technological fields, at all levels.

ORT colleges operate as interdisciplinary education centers, ranging from science and technology studies to the arts, design and communications. The colleges aim to provide a solution for every student, enabling them to successfully meet local and global technological requirements.

currentPositions.description:
Moved my unit into the AI world and achieved complete systems clarity for meaningful, measurable insights and impact. 
Owned end-to-end platform architecture and system design, research-driven technical strategy, and innovation across a portfolio (Volcano, Onyx, Sherlock + S.A.M classified).

•	Architected platform foundations for air-gapped, multi-network distributed systems across 6 private networks, defining service boundaries, contracts, integration patterns, and non-functional requirements (scalability, reliability, security, operability).

•	Designed DRP + resilience architecture: backups and recovery planning, including active-active thinking and multi-cluster architecture patterns proven in practice.

•	Led architecture for high-throughput telemetry infrastructure as part of the platform ecosystem (Volcano domain).

•	Drove architecture via a research-first approach: scored requirements, evaluated alternatives (protocols, brokers, databases, consistency patterns), ran targeted POCs, and documented final decisions in Confluence to keep architecture repeatable and future-proof.

•	Owned discovery → design → delivery: stakeholder workshops/surveys → system designs and DRs → execution plans, roadmaps, and phased rollouts.

•	Ran delivery like an architect-lead: Jira sprints, prioritization, retros, execution breakdowns; ensured implementation matched architecture and real operational constraints.

•	Expanded the team to full-stack delivery and introduced a consistent decision “triangle”:
    1.	best for the system
    2.	best for the client
    3.	future-proof + innovative.

•	AI innovation: initiated and led AI enablement for internal platforms (LLM/RAG direction as part of the architecture roadmap), accelerating knowledge access and operational workflows (details in Projects).

•	Technical leadership (non-managerial): aligned interfaces across engineers/teams, mentored, standardized practices, and raised the bar on design quality.
currentPositions.title: Solution Architect & Technical Lead

Afeka Tel Aviv Academic College of Engineering
Welcome to the Afeka Tel Aviv Academic College of Engineering!

Located at the heart of the Israeli high-tech industry in Tel Aviv, the Afeka Academic College of Engineering has positioned itself as one of the leading institutions for engineering studies in Israel. Afeka offers a strong and cutting-edge academic program that produces highly skilled and professional engineers. The exceptional level of training, coupled with Afeka's strong ties to the industry, paves the way for Afeka graduates to position themselves at the forefront of the Israeli and International engineering community.

The Afeka Academic College of Engineering is accredited by the Israel Council for Higher Education and offers B.Sc. degrees in the following fields of engineering:

Medical Engineering
Electrical Engineering
Mechanical Engineering
Industrial Engineering and Management
Software Engineering
Computer Science
Data Science


M.Sc. Program in:
Medical Engineering
Intelligent Systems
Systems Engineering
Energy and Power Systems Engineering
Industrial engineering and management

Welcome to the Jerusalem College of Technology (JCT), a leading institution where technological innovation meets traditional Judaic studies in the heart of Jerusalem. At JCT, we provide world-class education in technology, health sciences and business, coupled with a rich Judaic studies program that prepares students for leadership in a globalized world.

About Us:

At JCT, we are committed to excellence in education. Our programs in engineering, computer science, health sciences, business, and more are designed to equip students with cutting-edge technical skills and a strong ethical foundation. Our unique blend of modern technology and Jewish values offers a distinctive educational experience.

Why choose JCT?

Top-Tier Education: Our esteemed faculty deliver high-quality instruction in various fields, preparing our students for their future careers.

Comprehensive Judaic Studies: Students don’t have to choose between academic excellence and spiritual growth. Our Judaic studies program is designed to enhance both. Our top-level shiurim and rich religious environment allow students to deepen their spiritual and ethical development while pursuing their academic and professional goals.

Strategic Location: Situated in the historic and vibrant city of Jerusalem, JCT provides a stimulating academic environment enriched by cultural, spiritual and intellectual opportunities.

Supportive Community: We offer a collaborative community where students, faculty, and alumni support each other. Our focus on mentorship and career development ensures that students have the resources they need to excel.

Connect with us to learn more about our programs, events, and opportunities.

Stay Connected: Follow our LinkedIn page for updates, news, and insights. Visit our website or contact us for more information.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ono Academic College was established in 1995 as a private, non-profit academic institution and has set itself two principal goals:

A. Developing and studying applied academic programs, which will enable graduates to integrate directly into the labor market, while maintaining the highest level of academic standards and teaching quality.

B. Giving top priority to the social problems of the State of Israel, by finding solutions for various sectors of the population, providing the opportunity for higher academic studies and, as a result, the ability to play an active role in the Israeli workforce.

Within this framework, we have developed study courses for outstanding students, enabling them to fulfill their potential and devote their time to multidisciplinary studies in more than one faculty, according to their choice.
In addition, Ono is proud to be the only higher education institute in Israel to include four academic hours of compulsory community work. We regard this personal commitment as an educational message of the highest level, which will result in the students' appreciation of Ono Academic College's worldview. Developing social sensitivity and awareness of the needs of one's community is an integral part of training future lawyers, accountants, and business professionals.

Ono Academic College is home to over 23,000 active students currently enrolled in various programs, including the undergraduate program in Law (LLB) and the undergraduate and graduate programs in Business Administration (BA and MBA) with a variety of specializations: Marketing and Advertising, Finance, Information Systems Analysis, Accounting, and Human Resources Management.

To date, over 60,000 graduates have successfully completed their studies at Ono Academic College and have gone on to make a significant impact in their respective fields.

We wish you success in your application for acceptance and hope to see you among us in the coming academic year.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Ono Academic College

Stay Connected: Follow our LinkedIn page for updates, news, and insights. Visit our website or contact us for more information.

resumePosition.details:
• Designed enterprise integration architectures across Azure-based and hybrid cloud environments connecting CRM, EMR, operational systems, and AI-driven automation platforms.

• Collaborated with infrastructure, security, DevOps, and external vendors to define end-to-end architecture solutions.
• Conducted technical POCs and vendor evaluations to support architectural decisions.

currentPositions.description:
- 𝐌𝐢𝐜𝐫𝐨𝐬𝐞𝐫𝐯𝐢𝐜𝐞𝐬 𝐚𝐫𝐜𝐡𝐢𝐭𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞: Spearheaded the engineering and architectural design of an application deployed across avionics computers in various aircraft platforms, as well as ground-based systems.
This encompassed the intricate process of transitioning from a monolithic architecture to a microservices-based one. Key responsibilities included the meticulous selection of cutting-edge technologies, POC, navigating resource constraints, managing real-time data streams, ensuring rapid processing rates, and implementing robust synchronization mechanismsת & more
The technological landscape I navigated comprised a diverse array of tools and frameworks, including React, Node.js, .NET 7, MongoDB, Redis, Kubernetes (OpenShift 4), Splunk, RabbitMQ & more.
- 𝐌𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐨𝐫𝐬𝐡𝐢𝐩 & 𝐋𝐞𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬𝐡𝐢𝐩: Provided technical guidance and mentorship to developers spanning multiple teams, cultivating skill enhancement and advocating for best practices in software engineering. Additionally, in a supplementary capacity, I mentored software engineers, wherein I devised an architectural framework for their final projects aligned with future system requirements. This framework streamlined the integration of their projects into our system, ensuring seamless efficiency.
- 𝐂𝐥𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐑𝐞𝐪𝐮𝐢𝐫𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐓𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐬𝐥𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧: Proficiently translated clients' needs into actionable features, adeptly transforming conceptual requirements into tangible solutions, Expertise in crafting professional system specifications to ensure clarity and alignment with client expectations.
